name: Huang Qi (Astragalus)
benefit: Qi tonic; strengthens wei qi (immune defense), used in fatigue and recurrent illness.
recipe: 10–15 g root in decoction daily; or sliced root simmered in soups.
sourcing: Root of astragalus; northern China primary source.

name: Dang Gui
benefit: Chief blood-tonifying herb for women; menstrual regulation and postpartum recovery.
recipe: 6–12 g in decoction; commonly in Si Wu Tang formula.
sourcing: Root; Gansu and Yunnan provinces prized.

name: Goji (Gou Qi Zi)
benefit: Yin tonic for liver and kidney; eye health, longevity, blood building.
recipe: 10–15 g dried berries daily — in tea, porridge, or soup.

name: Chinese Yam (Shan Yao)
benefit: Spleen and lung tonic; mild and nourishing.
sourcing: Fresh tuber or dried slices; Henan prized.

name: Lily Bulb (Bai He)
benefit: Moistens lung yin, calms shen (spirit); dry cough and insomnia.

name: Jujube (Da Zao)
benefit: Tonifies spleen qi, calms spirit, harmonizes formulas.

name: Schisandra (Wu Wei Zi)
benefit: Five-flavor berry; adaptogen, liver protective, astringent.
recipe: 2–6 g berries in tea or decoction.
sourcing: Dried berries; Northeast China and Russia.

name: Eleuthero
benefit: Adaptogen; improves stamina and stress resilience. Formerly 'Siberian ginseng.'
recipe: 2–4 g root or standardized tincture daily.

name: Licorice (Gan Cao)
benefit: Harmonizes formulas, tonifies qi, soothes. Most-used herb in TCM.
recipe: 3–10 g in most decoctions. Caution with hypertension.

name: Ginger (Sheng Jiang)
benefit: Warms middle jiao, resolves wind-cold, anti-nausea. See main ginger entry.

name: Cinnamon Twig (Gui Zhi)
benefit: Warms channels, releases exterior wind-cold; cornerstone of Gui Zhi Tang.

name: Cinnamon Bark (Rou Gui)
benefit: Tonifies kidney yang, warms interior; used in cold-deficient conditions.

name: White Peony (Bai Shao)
benefit: Nourishes blood, calms liver yang, relieves cramps.
recipe: 6–15 g root in decoctions; often with licorice (Shao Yao Gan Cao Tang).

name: Red Peony (Chi Shao)
benefit: Moves blood, clears heat, used in blood stasis conditions.

name: Rehmannia Raw (Sheng Di Huang)
benefit: Clears heat, cools blood, nourishes yin; fever conditions.

name: Rehmannia Prepared (Shu Di Huang)
benefit: Tonifies kidney yin and blood; cornerstone of Liu Wei Di Huang Wan.

name: Cornus Fruit (Shan Zhu Yu)
benefit: Tonifies and secures kidney and liver; night sweats, spermatorrhea.

name: Poria (Fu Ling)
benefit: Drains dampness, strengthens spleen, calms spirit.

name: Atractylodes White (Bai Zhu)
benefit: Tonifies spleen qi, dries dampness; Si Jun Zi Tang.

name: Codonopsis (Dang Shen)
benefit: Mild qi tonic; often substituted for ren shen (ginseng) in formulas.

name: American Ginseng (Xi Yang Shen)
benefit: Cooling qi and yin tonic; for yin-deficiency heat with fatigue.
recipe: 3–6 g sliced root steeped or decocted.
sourcing: Cultivated in North America, prized Wisconsin.

name: Ren Shen (Asian Ginseng)
benefit: See main ginseng entry.

name: San Qi (Notoginseng)
benefit: Stops bleeding, moves blood, used in trauma and cardiovascular support.
recipe: 1–3 g powder daily; or in Yunnan Bai Yao formula.

name: Salvia Root (Dan Shen)
benefit: Moves blood, treats angina; extensively studied in cardiovascular trials.
recipe: 9–30 g in decoctions; or standardized tablets.

name: Chuan Xiong
benefit: Moves blood and qi; classical herb for headaches and menstrual pain.

name: Safflower (Hong Hua)
benefit: Invigorates blood, resolves stasis; amenorrhea, trauma.
recipe: 3–10 g in decoctions. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Peach Seed (Tao Ren)
benefit: Breaks blood stasis, moistens intestines.

name: Mulberry Leaf (Sang Ye)
benefit: Clears lung heat, cools liver; early-stage colds and dry cough.

name: Chrysanthemum (Ju Hua)
benefit: Clears liver heat, brightens eyes; headaches and red eyes.

name: Honeysuckle (Jin Yin Hua)
benefit: Clears heat and toxins; wind-heat colds and sore throats.

name: Forsythia (Lian Qiao)
benefit: Clears heat and toxins; paired with honeysuckle in Yin Qiao San.

name: Ban Lan Gen
benefit: Clears heat and toxins; widely used for sore throat and viral colds.

name: Da Qing Ye
benefit: Stronger antiviral and anti-heat than Ban Lan Gen; mumps, sore throat.

name: Huang Lian
benefit: Clears damp-heat; gastrointestinal infections, berberine-rich.

name: Huang Qin
benefit: Clears heat and fire; baicalin-rich, anti-inflammatory.

name: Huang Bai
benefit: Clears damp-heat from lower jiao; urinary tract and skin infections.

name: Xuan Shen
benefit: Nourishes yin, clears heat from blood; throat and skin inflammation.

name: Mai Men Dong
benefit: Moistens lung and stomach yin; dry cough, constipation from dryness.

name: Sha Shen
benefit: Moistens lung yin; dry cough and thirst after illness.

name: Tian Men Dong
benefit: Nourishes kidney and lung yin; chronic dry cough.

name: Yu Zhu
benefit: Nourishes stomach yin and moistens dryness; dry mouth, chronic cough.

name: Huang Jing
benefit: Tonifies qi, yin, and essence; longevity herb in Taoism.

name: He Shou Wu
benefit: Tonifies blood and essence; traditional hair-blackening herb. Prepared form only.
recipe: 9–15 g prepared root in decoction. Raw form toxic.

name: Du Zhong
benefit: Tonifies kidney and liver; lower-back pain, hypertension.

name: Xu Duan
benefit: Tonifies liver and kidney; strengthens bones and sinews.

name: Dang Shen
benefit: Duplicate of Codonopsis.

name: Chen Pi (Aged Tangerine Peel)
benefit: Regulates qi, dries dampness, aids digestion.
recipe: 3–9 g aged peel in decoctions or teas.
sourcing: Aged peel — older is better; Xinhui prized.

name: Zhi Shi
benefit: Strongly moves qi and resolves food stagnation.

name: Magnolia Bark (Hou Po)
benefit: Moves qi, dries dampness, relieves anxiety and fullness.

name: Pinellia (Ban Xia)
benefit: Transforms phlegm, descends stomach qi; must be processed.

name: Platycodon (Jie Geng)
benefit: Opens lungs, guides herbs upward; sore throat and cough.

name: Bei Mu (Fritillaria)
benefit: Transforms phlegm and stops cough; dry and productive cough.

name: Bitter Apricot (Xing Ren)
benefit: Stops cough, moistens intestines.
sourcing: Bitter apricot kernel — must be processed.

name: Loquat Leaf (Pi Pa Ye)
benefit: Clears lung heat, stops cough; used in classic Pi Pa Gao syrup.
recipe: 6–12 g leaves in decoction or syrup.

name: Ma Huang
benefit: Releases exterior wind-cold, warms lungs; classical asthma herb.
recipe: 3–10 g in formulas only; professional use.
sourcing: Stem; banned as supplement in US/EU.

name: Zi Su Ye (Perilla)
benefit: Releases exterior wind-cold, regulates qi; mild cold with cough.

name: Bo He (Field Mint)
benefit: Releases wind-heat, clears head; sore throat with fever.

name: Bai Zhi
benefit: Opens nasal passages, relieves sinus pain; wind-cold with headache.

name: Ge Gen (Kudzu)
benefit: Releases muscles, raises yang; hangover, neck tension, diarrhea.

name: Chai Hu
benefit: Spreads liver qi, harmonizes shao yang; depression, alternating chills/fever.

name: Bai Guo (Ginkgo)
benefit: Astringes lung, stops wheezing; seed toxic in excess, leaf more commonly used.
recipe: 5–10 g processed seed; or standardized leaf extract 120–240 mg.
sourcing: Seed or leaf; leaf extract most used.

name: Mu Xiang
benefit: Regulates qi, relieves abdominal pain; cornerstone digestive herb.

name: Yi Yi Ren (Job's Tears)
benefit: Drains dampness, strengthens spleen; food and medicine.
recipe: 9–30 g seeds in porridge or decoction.

name: Mung Bean (Lu Dou)
benefit: Clears summer heat and toxins; cooling food-medicine.
recipe: Mung bean soup with rock sugar for summer heat.

name: Lotus Seed (Lian Zi)
benefit: Tonifies spleen, calms shen; chronic diarrhea, insomnia, spermatorrhea.
recipe: 6–15 g seed in soups and porridges.

name: Reishi (Ling Zhi)
benefit: See main reishi entry.

name: Cordyceps (Dong Chong Xia Cao)
benefit: Tonifies lung and kidney, enhances stamina.
sourcing: Wild is extremely expensive; militaris cultivated strain reliable.

name: Turkey Tail (Yun Zhi)
benefit: Polysaccharide-rich immune modulator; oncology-adjunct studied.
recipe: 2–3 g dried mushroom powder or PSK/PSP extracts.

name: Lion's Mane (Hou Tou Gu)
benefit: Neurotrophic; cognition and nerve regeneration studies.
recipe: 1–3 g dried fruiting body extract daily.

name: Maitake
benefit: Beta-glucan-rich immune modulator; blood-sugar support.
recipe: 1–3 g daily in capsules or culinary use.

name: Shiitake
benefit: Lentinan polysaccharide; immune and cardiovascular support.
recipe: 3–5 dried caps in soups several times weekly.

name: Gui Ban (Tortoise Plastron)
benefit: Nourishes yin; traditional strong yin tonic. CITES concerns.

name: Wu Ling San
benefit: Classical diuretic; edema, urinary dysfunction.
recipe: 6–12 g granules or decoction as directed.

name: Si Jun Zi Tang
benefit: Foundational spleen qi tonic: Ren Shen, Bai Zhu, Fu Ling, Gan Cao.

name: Liu Wei Di Huang Wan
benefit: Foundational kidney-yin tonic; tinnitus, night sweats, vertigo.

name: Xiao Yao San
benefit: Classical liver-qi regulating formula; PMS, mood swings, fatigue with frustration.

name: Gui Pi Tang
benefit: Tonifies qi and blood, calms spirit; insomnia with rumination, fatigue.

name: Yin Qiao San
benefit: Wind-heat release; early-stage sore throat with fever. Famous cold formula.
recipe: Tablets taken at first symptom onset, every 2–4 hr.

name: Yunnan Baiyao
benefit: Stops bleeding, resolves stasis; trauma, surgery adjunct.
recipe: 0.25–0.5 g oral; or topical sprinkle on wounds.
